ddls:
CREATE TABLE "servers" (
  "server_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each server */,
  "server_name" TEXT /* Name of the server */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the server */,
  "operating_system" TEXT /* Operating system running on the server */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address of the server */,
  "server_type" TEXT /* Type of server (e.g., physical, virtual) */,
  "hardware_model" TEXT /* Model of the server hardware */,
  "total_storage" REAL /* Total storage capacity */,
  "last_maintenance" TEXT /* Date and time of the last maintenance */,
  "next_maintenance" TEXT /* Date and time of the next scheduled maintenance */,
  "maintenance_history" TEXT /* History of maintenance activities */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("server_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "alerts" (
  "alert_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each alert */,
  "metric_id" INTEGER /* ID of the metric that triggered the alert */,
  "alert_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was generated */,
  "alert_type" TEXT /* Type of alert (e.g., high CPU, low memory) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the alert */,
  "severity" TEXT /* Severity level of the alert (e.g., critical, warning, info) */,
  "resolved" INTEGER /* Whether the alert has been resolved */,
  "resolution_time" TEXT /* Time when the alert was resolved */,
  "resolved_by" TEXT /* User who resolved the alert */,
  "alert_description" TEXT /* Detailed description of the alert */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("alert_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., admin, engineer) */,
  "server_access" TEXT /* Servers the user has access to */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for security */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Last login time */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Whether the user account is active */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Account creation date and time */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last account update date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Role creation date and time */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last role update date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_roles_permissions FOREIGN KEY ("permissions") REFERENCES permissions ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "created_at" TEXT /* Permission creation date and time */,
  "updated_at" TEXT /* Last permission update date and time */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)
column_names: Operating system running on the server
